questions.question: What is the per-rank bandwidth cost of ring allreduce for a tensor of T bytes on N ranks?
questions.explanation: Ring allreduce moves N-1 chunks of size T/N twice (reduce-scatter then allgather); the per-rank total is 2T(N-1)/N, independent of cluster size.

questions.question: Why is the queue mesh in this lesson an honest stand-in for NCCL on a real cluster?
options: It implements the same wire pattern (ordered point-to-point sends around a ring), only the medium differs
questions.explanation: Algorithmic correctness depends on the wire pattern, not the medium; the queue ring exposes the same race shapes and chunk schedule as NCCL ring allreduce.

questions.question: After the reduce-scatter half of ring allreduce, what does rank r hold?
options: The reduced sum for one chunk (one Nth of the tensor)
The reduced sum for two chunks
questions.explanation: Reduce-scatter parks the sum of one chunk on one rank; the allgather half then rotates those completed chunks around the ring.

questions.question: Why use tree broadcast instead of N-1 direct sends from the source?
options: Trees double the holder set per round; depth is log2(N) so latency scales as log not N
questions.explanation: log2(N) hops dominate when latency per hop is the bottleneck; N-1 sequential sends pay that latency floor N times.

questions.question: Why verify against torch.distributed gloo on the same input?
options: Gloo is the legal reference for collective semantics; byte-equal output proves your algorithm agrees with the spec before it runs on a real cluster
questions.explanation: Without a reference, the primitive looks correct until step 10000 of a real training run; gloo gives byte-equal ground truth on CPU.

questions.question: When does tree allreduce win over ring allreduce?
options: Small messages or high-latency links; the log2(N) hop count beats the bandwidth term 2T(N-1)/N below the crossover
questions.explanation: NCCL picks ring above ~1 MB and tree below; the crossover is bandwidth-versus-latency on the given cluster topology.
